description: Organic mycelium-like growth patterns: curl noise advects Worley cell centres over time, producing branching tendrils. Ridged multifractal FBM adds surface roughness suggesting hyphal texture.

base.comment: Base Worley cells become the nucleation sites of fungal colonies

modifiers.comment: Curl advection bends cell edges into tendril shapes
Ridged FBM adds hyphal surface texture and branching ridge detail
Soft threshold separates mycelium mass from substrate

example_prompts: create a fungal mycelium growth pattern with branching tendrils driven by curl noise
make a biological spore colony texture using curl-advected Worley cells and ridged FBM

screenshot_metadata.notes: Render at 1024x1024. Use earthy tones: dark soil brown (#1a0f07) as substrate, pale mycelium white (#e8e2d0) for hyphae, with faint bioluminescent green (#4aff8c) highlights. Animate slowly at 0.08 time scale.
